metadata.title: Tailwind CSS spacing to pixels — p-4 to px converter
metadata.description: See exact pixel values for any Tailwind spacing class. p-4 = 16px, p-8 = 32px, w-64 = 256px — free, no registration, works in browser.

hero.title: Convert Tailwind spacing to px – p-4, w-48, gap-8 values

howTo.name: How to use the Tailwind to px converter

steps.name: Reference table
steps.text: Check the table below for common values.

contentBlocks.title: When do you need to convert Tailwind units to pixels?
contentBlocks.html:
Tailwind CSS uses a spacing scale where each unit = 0.25 rem = 4 px (at default 16px root). So p-4 = 1 rem = 16 px, p-8 = 2 rem = 32 px, w-64 = 16 rem = 256 px.

This converter helps developers quickly translate Tailwind spacing values to exact pixel measurements for debugging, design handoff, and documentation.

Tailwind also has special values: 0.5 = 2px, 1.5 = 6px, 2.5 = 10px, 3.5 = 14px, and named sizes like full = 100%, screen = 100vw.

All calculations run locally in your browser — nothing is sent to any server.

items.title: 1. Enter a value
items.description: Type a number in the input field. You can use a period or comma as the decimal separator.

items.title: 2. Read the result
items.description: The conversion result appears instantly in the field next to it – no clicking required.

items.title: 3. Copy or reverse
items.description: Click Copy result or use the Reverse button to convert in the opposite direction.

items.title: CSS debugging and class lookup
items.description: During development you often need to confirm: how many pixels is gap-6? Is max-w-md wider than 400px? Instead of opening Tailwind docs and scrolling, type the class number here and get the answer in one second. p-6 = 24px, gap-6 = 24px, max-w-md = 448px.

items.title: Figma to Tailwind design handoff
items.description: Figma designs are specified in pixels. When converting a design to Tailwind, you need to find the nearest spacing token. A 32px gap in Figma maps to gap-8 in Tailwind (8 × 4px = 32px). This tool lets you verify the reverse — type the class, confirm the pixel value matches the spec.

items.title: Component sizing with shadcn/ui
items.description: shadcn/ui components use Tailwind spacing tokens for height, padding, and radius. A standard button uses h-10 (40px), px-4 (16px), py-2 (8px). When you need to override or extend these for a custom design, knowing the exact pixel equivalent prevents layout shifts.

items.title: Custom spacing configuration
items.description: When you extend the Tailwind spacing scale in tailwind.config.js under theme.extend.spacing, you define new tokens like spacing['4.5'] = '18px'. This converter helps verify that your custom values align with the existing 4px grid and do not create rounding inconsistencies across components.

items.title: Complete privacy
items.description: All calculations run locally in your browser. No data is sent to any server.

items.title: Real-time results
items.description: The result updates instantly as you type – no need to click any button.

items.title: Bidirectional conversion
items.description: The Reverse button lets you convert in both directions with one click.

items.title: Reference table
items.description: A ready-made table with commonly converted values and contextual descriptions.

contentBlocks.title: How does Tailwind spacing work?
contentBlocks.html:
Tailwind spacing = value × 0.25 rem. At 16px root: spacing × 4 = pixels.

p-1 = 4px, p-2 = 8px, p-4 = 16px, p-8 = 32px, p-16 = 64px, p-32 = 128px, p-64 = 256px.

Font sizes use different values: text-sm = 14px, text-base = 16px, text-lg = 18px, text-xl = 20px, text-2xl = 24px.

contentBlocks.title: Practical tips
contentBlocks.html:
• Spacing: p-1 = 4px, p-2 = 8px, p-3 = 12px, p-4 = 16px, p-6 = 24px, p-8 = 32px.
  • Width: w-1 = 4px, w-4 = 16px, w-12 = 48px, w-24 = 96px, w-48 = 192px, w-64 = 256px.
  • Breakpoints: sm ≥ 640px, md ≥ 768px, lg ≥ 1024px, xl ≥ 1280px.
  • Max-width: max-w-sm = 384px, max-w-md = 448px, max-w-lg = 512px, max-w-xl = 576px.

items.question: What is p-4 in pixels?
items.answer: p-4 = 1rem = 16px at the default root font size of 16px. The Tailwind spacing formula is: px = unit × 4. So p-4 = 4 × 4 = 16px, p-8 = 32px, p-12 = 48px.

items.question: How do Tailwind spacing units work?
items.answer: Each spacing unit = 0.25rem. At 16px root, multiply by 4 to get pixels. p-6 = 6 × 4 = 24px, w-24 = 24 × 4 = 96px, gap-2 = 2 × 4 = 8px. Tailwind also has half-unit values: p-0.5 = 2px, p-1.5 = 6px, p-2.5 = 10px.

items.question: What are Tailwind breakpoints in pixels?
items.answer: Tailwind v3/v4 default breakpoints: sm = 640px, md = 768px, lg = 1024px, xl = 1280px, 2xl = 1536px. These are min-width breakpoints, so md:p-4 applies at 768px and above.

items.question: Can I customize the Tailwind spacing scale?
items.answer: Yes. In tailwind.config.js under theme.spacing (to fully replace the default scale) or theme.extend.spacing (to add new values while keeping defaults). JIT mode in Tailwind v3+ also supports arbitrary values like p-[13px] without config changes.

items.question: What are the most common Tailwind spacing values I should know?
How do I convert Figma spacing values to Tailwind classes?
items.answer: Divide the Figma pixel value by 4 to get the Tailwind unit. 32px / 4 = 8, so use p-8 or gap-8 or m-8. If the value does not divide evenly (e.g. 14px), use the closest token (p-3 = 12px or p-4 = 16px) or an arbitrary value: p-[14px].

items.question: Does Tailwind use rem or px internally?
items.answer: Tailwind v3 uses rem for all spacing and font sizes internally. 1 unit = 0.25rem. At 16px root: 1 unit = 4px. If you change the root font size, all Tailwind rem values shift accordingly — which is why this converter accepts a custom base size.

items.question: Are calculations done locally?
items.answer: Yes. All calculations run in your browser. No data is sent to any server.
